name: Qalam Education Fund
mission:
Inspiring growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation.

We envision a community that thrives off of accessible Islamic knowledge and...
theoryOfChange: Qalam’s approach is to inspire growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation, connecting what they impart back to the Holy Qur’an and the Prophetic tradition, with a three-pronged approach: Ta'leem (learning of sacred knowledge), Tarbiya (personal growth and development), and Suhba (mentorship and companionship).
scoreSummary: Qalam Education Fund Inc shows good alignment and limited impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.qalam.institute/zakat (Source: https://www.qalam.institute/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 110 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 31/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions the program expense ratio and diverse programs but doesn't adequately address the significant deductions due to excessive working capital, weak governance (small board), and lack of beneficiary data for cost-per-beneficiary assessment. These factors heavily contribute to the low impact score and should be explicitly mentioned.

The overall amal_score rationale mentions strong financial health and efficiency, but this is misleading given the significant risk deductions and the 'ELEVATED' overall risk level. The rationale should acknowledge the financial risks associated with excessive working capital and the operational risks due to the small board size.
BBB name 'Tree Research and Education Endowment Fund' may not match 'Qalam Education Fund' (similarity: 40%)

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

risks.description: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows a medium alignment for Muslim donors with a score of 31/50, supported by its zakat-eligible status and focus on global education [2][7]. Its mission to provide accessible Islamic knowledge aligns with urgent community needs for religious literacy and leadership training [5][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: Qalam demonstrates a commitment to its mission by maintaining a program expense ratio of 81.2% and significant working capital of 109.7 months [1][3]. While it successfully operates diverse programs like the Qalam Seminary and Podcast, the impact score of 18/50 reflects its primary role in capacity building rather than direct service delivery with standardized cost-per-beneficiary data [4][5].

strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.2% ensuring most funds reach educational initiatives
Exceptional fundraising efficiency at $0.01 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 109.7 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Qalam Education Fund Inc is a non-profit organization that provides Islamic education through its Seminary, Podcast, and Qur’an Intensive programs [4][5]. The organization reported a total revenue of $2,329,468 and maintains a policy for zakat-eligible contributions to support its educational mission [1][2][3].

confidence.notes: Financial efficiency is high, but governance and impact transparency are significant gaps.

alignment.explanation: Strong alignment for Muslim donors seeking <cite id="7">zakat-eligible educational initiatives</cite> with a <cite id="1">17-year track record</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Clarify the <cite id="7">zakat allocation policy</cite> to specify exactly how student tuition or scholarships are funded via zakat.

credibility.explanation: Donors can verify Qalam's financial standing through <cite id="3">ProPublica's Form 990 data</cite>, though credibility is tempered by <cite id="12">failing 7 of 20 BBB standards</cite> related to policy documentation.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="11">expand its board size</cite> beyond two members to improve governance and oversight.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by <cite id="4">diverse educational programs</cite> and a <cite id="5">100.0% program ratio</cite>, though <cite id="13">cost-per-beneficiary data is currently missing</cite>.
impact.improvement: Publish <cite id="13">consistent beneficiary counts</cite> so that the cost-effectiveness of the Seminary and Podcast programs can be assessed.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term infrastructure of Islamic education and value high-growth organizations with minimal fundraising overhead.
donor_motivations: Want to support the production of free Islamic digital content (podcasts)
Prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising costs
Seek to fulfill zakat through educational scholarships and seminary support
giving_considerations: The organization holds a very large reserve of cash relative to its annual spending
Governance is centralized within a very small board of directors
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors who prioritize organizations with large, independent boards or those requiring third-party verified impact metrics.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a clear strategic plan but <cite id="13">no published data on student outcomes</cite> or cost-per-beneficiary.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Qalam creates impact by training future leaders through its Seminary and providing accessible knowledge to the masses via digital media.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational programs are effectively increasing the religious literacy of the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While revenue is growing rapidly, the <cite id="8">large asset base</cite> suggests the org has significant existing capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">massive digital footprint via the Qalam Podcast</cite> and high revenue growth.

strengths.detail: The organization allocates a <cite id="5">81.2% program expense ratio</cite>, maximizing the impact of donor contributions on educational delivery.
Qalam demonstrates elite fiscal discipline with a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="8">109.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing extreme long-term stability for its academic programs.

strengths_deep_dive: Qalam's financial model is characterized by rapid expansion and low overhead. Since its founding in <cite id="1">2009</cite>, it has scaled to a <cite id="2">$2.3 million revenue base</cite> while maintaining a lean administrative structure. Its grantmaking activity also supports local community infrastructure, with <cite id="9">$414,289 in total grants</cite> distributed to domestic partners like Roots Community Inc and the Al-Qalam Foundation. This dual approach of direct education and community support is bolstered by a <cite id="10">clear theory of change</cite> focused on nurturing a thriving community through sacred knowledge.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">2009</cite>, Qalam Education Fund Inc is an educational institution dedicated to providing accessible Islamic knowledge through a variety of academic and community-based programs. The organization reported <cite id="2">$2,338,320 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year, demonstrating significant growth with a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 568.6%</cite>. Qalam operates several flagship initiatives, including the <cite id="4">Qalam Seminary, the Qalam Podcast, and specialized intensives</cite> focused on the Qur’an and Seerah. Financially, the organization maintains a <cite id="5">81.2% program expense ratio</cite> according to its most recent filings, ensuring that all functional expenses are directed toward its educational mission. Furthermore, the charity operates with <cite id="6">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only $0.01 to raise every $1 in contributions. As a <cite id="7">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, Qalam facilitates the distribution of funds to eligible categories such as the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin) within its educational framework.
